We were looking for a quick and short hike in Griffith Park.  This was perfect.  Parked our car in the small parking lot, no fee.  We walked the short trail up to the Bronson Cave.  It is more like a tunnel, now fenced off probably due to instability of the surrounding hills.  

Originally a rock quarry in Griffith Park that provided materials for early city infrastructure like Wilshire Boulevard. The quarry closed in the late 1920s, and the man-made tunnel became a popular filming location, most famously as the "Batcave" and used for Star Trek.  We saw a huge coyote on our way up that jogged off into the bushes.  Supposedly this area is mountain lion country too.  That is a bit unsettling.

One bonus we didn't realize and no one mentioned:  on our way down from looking at the back side of the tunnel, you get an amazing view to the Hollywood sign!!  Way closer from the Griffith Observatory.  We were quite pleased with the bonus view.

It's a very quick walk (< 10 minutes) from the parking lot to the caves, which themselves are pretty small and gated off. I was struck by how silent and detached the area was even though I entered through a little suburban neighborhood. I probably would have appreciated the caves more if I could name any particular movies they were featured in - I simply knew they were famous somehow and wanted to check them out. Worth going to bc of how quick it is to see them.
